cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-920 - Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

threat.title: Kafka Consumer Group Starvation Attack
threat.description: Attackers exploit Kafka's consumer group coordination mechanism to trigger cascading rebalance storms, partition flooding, and offset commit exhaustion, leading to message processing halts and complete denial of service. By manipulating consumer group joins/leaves, flooding partition keys, or exploiting fetch.max.bytes limits, malicious actors can starve legitimate consumers and cause critical data processing pipelines to fail.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker establishes connection to Kafka cluster without authentication or using compromised credentials
2. Creates malicious consumer group with high session.timeout.ms (300000ms) and low heartbeat.interval.ms (1000ms)
3. Rapidly joins and leaves consumer group (50+ times/minute) triggering continuous rebalance storms across all partitions
4. Floods specific partition keys with requests causing partition leader exhaustion and hot partition issues
5. Exploits fetch.max.bytes by requesting maximum message sizes (52428800 bytes) causing broker memory pressure
6. Legitimate consumers enter permanent rebalance state, consumer lag grows exponentially, message processing halts completely
prerequisites: Kafka cluster lacks network-level authentication (no SASL/SSL) OR attacker has valid credentials
No consumer group quotas configured (consumer_byte_rate, request_percentage unlimited)
Missing or inadequate broker-level quotas (producer_byte_rate, consumer_byte_rate, request_percentage)
ZooKeeper or KRaft coordination service accessible to attacker
No rate limiting on consumer group join/leave operations

cve_references.description: Memory exhaustion in Apache Kafka allows malicious unauthenticated clients to allocate large amounts of memory on brokers, leading to OutOfMemoryException and denial of service
SASL JAAS JndiLoginModule configuration vulnerability enabling RCE/DoS attacks via Kafka Connect API and brokers
JNDI Configuration RCE/DoS extending CVE-2023-25194 to Apache Kafka brokers requiring AlterConfigs permission

real_world_breaches.impact: Cascading failure in Kafka system disrupted event processing for customers in US Service Regions. Root cause: logical error creating new Kafka producer per API request instead of singleton pattern
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Kafka producer instantiation storm causing consumer group instability and processing delays

real_world_breaches.impact: Critical Kafka and ClickHouse services processing logs and analytics failed during data center outage due to single-region deployment
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Kafka cluster unavailability causing analytics pipeline complete shutdown

real_world_breaches.impact: Affected all Kafka releases since 2.8.0, allowing unauthenticated memory exhaustion attacks on brokers
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of validation on client request sizes enabling arbitrarily large memory allocations (CWE-400)

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Consumer group rebalance storm causing complete message processing halt via rapid join/leave cycles

security_research.title: Kafka Rebalance Storm: Production Incident Analysis
security_research.key_finding: In production, dozens of consumer groups can trigger rebalancing storms requiring urgent intervention. Development environments mask this issue with minimal coordination overhead.

security_research.title: Apache Kafka Issues in Production: How to Diagnose and Prevent Failures
security_research.key_finding: Consumer lag amplification and rebalance failures are among top causes of Kafka production outages

security_research.title: Disaster Recovery for Multi-Region Kafka at Uber
security_research.key_finding: Uber processes trillions of Kafka messages and petabytes of data daily, requiring sophisticated multi-region disaster recovery to prevent consumer group failures

mitigations.title: Implement Kafka Broker and Consumer Group Quotas
mitigations.description: Configure comprehensive quota controls at broker and consumer group levels to prevent resource exhaustion attacks. Use producer_byte_rate, consumer_byte_rate, request_percentage, and controller_mutation_rate quotas to throttle malicious clients.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Kafka Broker Quota Configuration (server.properties)

# === BROKER-LEVEL QUOTAS ===
# Default quota for all clients (10 MB/s producer, 50 MB/s consumer)
quota.producer.default=10485760
quota.consumer.default=52428800

# Request rate limiting (percentage of request handler threads)
quota.producer.request.percentage.default=50
quota.consumer.request.percentage.default=50

# Controller mutation rate (protect ZooKeeper/KRaft from storms)
quota.controller.mutation.rate=100

# Connection creation rate (prevent connection flooding)
max.connections.per.ip=100
max.connections.per.ip.overrides=127.0.0.1:200

# === CONSUMER GROUP SPECIFIC QUOTAS ===
# Using kafka-configs.sh to set quotas for specific consumer groups

# Limit high-risk consumer group to 5 MB/s
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'consumer_byte_rate=5242880,request_percentage=30' \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name high-risk-consumer

# Set quota for critical production consumer group (higher limits)
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'consumer_byte_rate=104857600,request_percentage=80' \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name production-consumer

# Prevent partition key flooding - set partition-level limits
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'max.message.bytes=1048576' \
  --entity-type topics \
  --entity-name critical-topic

# === CONSUMER GROUP HARDENING ===
# Consumer configuration (consumer.properties)

# Prevent rebalance storms
session.timeout.ms=30000
heartbeat.interval.ms=3000
max.poll.interval.ms=300000
max.poll.records=500

# Limit fetch sizes to prevent memory exhaustion
fetch.max.bytes=52428800
max.partition.fetch.bytes=1048576

# Enable consumer group isolation
group.instance.id=consumer-instance-001
isolation.level=read_committed

# === KRAFT MODE HARDENING (Kafka 3.3+) ===
# Replace ZooKeeper coordination with KRaft for better DoS resistance
process.roles=broker,controller
node.id=1
controller.quorum.voters=1@localhost:9093

# Controller request quotas
controller.quota.window.num=11
controller.quota.window.size.seconds=1

# === MONITORING QUOTA VIOLATIONS ===
# Query JMX metrics for quota throttle events
bin/kafka-run-class.sh kafka.tools.JmxTool \
  --object-name kafka.server:type=KafkaRequestHandlerPool,name=RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent \
  --reporting-interval 1000

# View consumer group lag and throttle status
bin/kafka-consumer-groups.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --describe \
  --group my-consumer-group \
  --state

# === DYNAMIC QUOTA UPDATES (No Restart Required) ===
# Temporarily throttle suspected malicious consumer
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'consumer_byte_rate=1048576' \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name suspected-attacker

# Verify quota configuration
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9092 \
  --describe \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name suspected-attacker

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Verify quota configuration in server.properties and via kafka-configs.sh --describe
Simulate high-volume consumer and confirm throttling occurs (monitor kafka.server:type=Fetch metrics)
Test consumer group rebalance behavior under quota constraints
Validate JMX metrics show ThrottleTimeMs > 0 for quota-limited clients
Confirm consumer lag monitoring alerts trigger when quotas cause backpressure

mitigations.title: Enable SASL/SSL Authentication and ACLs
mitigations.description: Implement strong authentication using SASL_SSL with SCRAM-SHA-512 and configure fine-grained ACLs to prevent unauthorized consumer group access and operations.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Kafka SASL/SSL Configuration for DoS Prevention

# === BROKER CONFIGURATION (server.properties) ===

# Enable SASL_SSL listener
listeners=SASL_SSL://0.0.0.0:9093
advertised.listeners=SASL_SSL://kafka-broker-1.example.com:9093

# SSL Configuration
ssl.keystore.location=/var/private/ssl/kafka.broker.keystore.jks
ssl.keystore.password=${SSL_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}
ssl.key.password=${SSL_KEY_PASSWORD}
ssl.truststore.location=/var/private/ssl/kafka.broker.truststore.jks
ssl.truststore.password=${SSL_TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD}

# Client authentication required
ssl.client.auth=required
ssl.endpoint.identification.algorithm=https

# SASL Configuration (SCRAM-SHA-512)
sasl.enabled.mechanisms=SCRAM-SHA-512
sasl.mechanism.inter.broker.protocol=SCRAM-SHA-512
inter.broker.listener.name=SASL_SSL

# Security protocol
security.inter.broker.protocol=SASL_SSL

# Enable ACL authorizer
authorizer.class.name=kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer
super.users=User:admin;User:kafka-broker
allow.everyone.if.no.acl.found=false

# === CREATE SASL USERS ===
bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'SCRAM-SHA-512=[password=strongpassword123!]' \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name legitimate-consumer

bin/kafka-configs.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --alter \
  --add-config 'SCRAM-SHA-512=[password=admin_secure_pass456!]' \
  --entity-type users \
  --entity-name admin

# === CONFIGURE FINE-GRAINED ACLS ===

# Allow consumer group to read from specific topic only
bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --add \
  --allow-principal User:legitimate-consumer \
  --operation Read \
  --topic production-events \
  --group production-consumer-group

# Deny consumer group operations for untrusted users
bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --add \
  --deny-principal User:untrusted-user \
  --operation All \
  --group '*'

# Prevent unauthorized topic creation (DoS via topic flooding)
bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --add \
  --deny-principal User:* \
  --allow-principal User:admin \
  --operation Create \
  --cluster

# Limit AlterConfigs to admin only (CVE-2025-27819 mitigation)
bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --add \
  --allow-principal User:admin \
  --operation AlterConfigs \
  --cluster

bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --add \
  --deny-principal User:* \
  --operation AlterConfigs \
  --cluster

# === CLIENT CONFIGURATION (consumer.properties) ===
bootstrap.servers=kafka-broker-1.example.com:9093
security.protocol=SASL_SSL
sasl.mechanism=SCRAM-SHA-512
sasl.jaas.config=org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule required \
  username="legitimate-consumer" \
  password="strongpassword123!";

# SSL trust store configuration
ssl.truststore.location=/var/private/ssl/kafka.client.truststore.jks
ssl.truststore.password=${SSL_TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD}
ssl.endpoint.identification.algorithm=https

# === DISABLE JNDI LOGIN MODULE (CVE-2025-27819/CVE-2023-25194) ===
# Add to JVM startup options
-Dorg.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules=com.sun.security.auth.module.JndiLoginModule,com.sun.security.auth.module.LdapLoginModule

# === LIST AND VERIFY ACLS ===
bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --list \
  --topic production-events

bin/kafka-acls.sh --bootstrap-server localhost:9093 \
  --command-config admin.properties \
  --list \
  --group production-consumer-group

nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited
validation_steps: Generate SSL certificates and configure truststore/keystore
Create SASL/SCRAM users with strong passwords
Test connection with valid credentials succeeds
Verify connection without credentials is denied (expect authentication failure)
Confirm ACLs block unauthorized consumer group operations
Validate JVM flag disables JNDI login modules (CVE mitigation)

mitigations.title: Deploy Consumer Lag Monitoring and Rebalance Detection
mitigations.description: Implement real-time monitoring for consumer lag amplification, rebalance storms, and partition assignment anomalies using Burrow, Prometheus JMX Exporter, and custom alerting.

code_example.code:
# docker-compose.yml for Kafka Monitoring Stack
version: '3.8'

services:
  # Burrow - Consumer Lag Monitoring (LinkedIn OSS)
  burrow:
    image: linkedin/burrow:latest
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - ./burrow.toml:/etc/burrow/burrow.toml
    environment:
      - BURROW_CONFIG=/etc/burrow/burrow.toml

  # Prometheus JMX Exporter for Kafka Metrics
  kafka-exporter:
    image: danielqsj/kafka-exporter:latest
    ports:
      - "9308:9308"
    command:
      - '--kafka.server=kafka-broker-1:9092'
      - '--kafka.server=kafka-broker-2:9092'
      - '--kafka.server=kafka-broker-3:9092'

  # Prometheus Server
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
      - prometheus-data:/prometheus
    command:
      - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
      - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d'

  # Grafana for Visualization
  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin
    volumes:
      - grafana-data:/var/lib/grafana
      - ./grafana-dashboards:/etc/grafana/provisioning/dashboards

volumes:
  prometheus-data:
  grafana-data:

---
# burrow.toml - Consumer Lag Monitoring Configuration
[general]
logdir = "/var/log/burrow"
loglevel = "info"

[zookeeper]
servers = ["zookeeper-1:2181", "zookeeper-2:2181", "zookeeper-3:2181"]
timeout = 6
root-path = "/burrow"

[kafka "local"]
brokers = ["kafka-broker-1:9092", "kafka-broker-2:9092", "kafka-broker-3:9092"]
topic-refresh = 60
offset-refresh = 30

[consumer "local"]
class-name = "kafka"
cluster = "local"
servers = ["kafka-broker-1:9092", "kafka-broker-2:9092"]
group-whitelist = ".*"

[httpserver.default]
address = ":8000"

# Evaluation rules for consumer lag alerts
[evaluator.default]
class-name = "caching"
expire-cache = 10

[evaluator.default.threshold]
warning = 1000
error = 5000

---
# prometheus.yml - Metrics Scraping Configuration
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'kafka-exporter'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['kafka-exporter:9308']

  - job_name: 'burrow'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['burrow:8000']
    metrics_path: '/v3/kafka'

  - job_name: 'kafka-jmx'
    static_configs:
      - targets: 
        - 'kafka-broker-1:9999'
        - 'kafka-broker-2:9999'
        - 'kafka-broker-3:9999'

# Alert Rules for Consumer Group DoS Detection
rule_files:
  - '/etc/prometheus/alerts/kafka-alerts.yml'

---
# alerts/kafka-alerts.yml - Prometheus Alert Rules
groups:
  - name: kafka_consumer_dos
    interval: 30s
    rules:
      # Consumer lag amplification attack
      - alert: ConsumerLagExplosion
        expr: |
          kafka_consumergroup_lag > 10000
          and
          rate(kafka_consumergroup_lag[5m]) > 1000
        for: 2m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Consumer group {{ $labels.consumergroup }} lag exploding"
          description: "Lag is {{ $value }} and growing at >1000 msgs/sec - potential DoS attack"

      # Rebalance storm detection
      - alert: ConsumerRebalanceStorm
        expr: |
          rate(kafka_consumer_group_rebalance_total[5m]) > 10
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Consumer group {{ $labels.consumergroup }} rebalancing {{ $value }} times/min"
          description: "Possible rebalance storm attack - investigate consumer join/leave patterns"

      # Partition fetch size exploitation
      - alert: SuspiciousFetchSize
        expr: |
          kafka_server_brokertopicmetrics_bytesinpersec{topic=~".*"} > 100000000
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Abnormal fetch size on topic {{ $labels.topic }}"
          description: "Fetch rate {{ $value }} bytes/sec may indicate fetch.max.bytes exploitation"

      # Offset commit flooding
      - alert: OffsetCommitFlooding
        expr: |
          rate(kafka_server_kafkarequesthandlerpool_requesthandleravgidlepercent[5m]) < 0.2
        for: 3m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Kafka request handlers saturated ({{ $value }} idle %)"
          description: "Possible offset commit flooding attack - handlers <20% idle"

      # Consumer group member churn
      - alert: ConsumerGroupChurn
        expr: |
          kafka_consumergroup_members < 1
          or
          rate(kafka_consumergroup_members[2m]) > 5
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Consumer group {{ $labels.consumergroup }} unstable membership"
          description: "Group has {{ $value }} members or high churn - check for join/leave attacks"

---
# Python script for custom Burrow lag alerting
# monitor_consumer_lag.py
import requests
import time
import json
from datetime import datetime

BURROW_API = "http://localhost:8000/v3/kafka"
CLUSTER = "local"
ALERT_THRESHOLD = 10000
ALERT_WEBHOOK = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL"

def check_consumer_groups():
    """Monitor all consumer groups for DoS indicators"""
    response = requests.get(f"{BURROW_API}/{CLUSTER}/consumer")
    consumer_groups = response.json()['consumers']
    
    for group in consumer_groups:
        lag_response = requests.get(f"{BURROW_API}/{CLUSTER}/consumer/{group}/lag")
        lag_data = lag_response.json()
        
        if lag_data['status']['status'] == 'ERR':
            total_lag = lag_data['status']['total_lag']
            
            if total_lag > ALERT_THRESHOLD:
                alert_msg = {
                    "text": f"🚨 KAFKA DOS ALERT: Consumer group {group} lag: {total_lag} messages",
                    "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                    "severity": "critical"
                }
                requests.post(ALERT_WEBHOOK, json=alert_msg)
                print(f"[ALERT] {group}: {total_lag} messages behind")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    while True:
        check_consumer_groups()
        time.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds

iso27001_controls: A.16.1.4 - Assessment of and decision on information security events
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources and sensors
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
RS.AN-1 - Notifications from detection systems are investigated
validation_steps: Deploy Burrow and verify it discovers all consumer groups
Configure Prometheus to scrape Kafka JMX metrics and Burrow API
Import Grafana dashboard for Kafka consumer monitoring
Simulate consumer lag by stopping consumer and verify alert fires
Test rebalance detection by rapidly joining/leaving consumer group
Validate Slack/PagerDuty webhook integration for critical alerts

indicators: Consumer lag growing exponentially (>10000 messages and increasing >1000 msgs/sec)
Consumer group rebalancing more than 10 times per 5 minutes
Abnormally large fetch.max.bytes requests (>50MB per request)
High offset commit rate with low message consumption rate
Consumer group member count oscillating rapidly (join/leave storm)
Broker request handler pool saturation (<20% idle)
ZooKeeper/KRaft connection spike from single IP address
Partition leader imbalance with hot partition issues
Consumer group coordinator failover events increasing

log_queries.query:
// Detect Kafka consumer group rebalance storms
KafkaLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(10m)
| where LogLevel == "WARN" or LogLevel == "ERROR"
| where Message contains "Rebalance" or Message contains "consumer group"
| summarize RebalanceCount = count() by ConsumerGroup, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where RebalanceCount > 10
| project TimeGenerated, ConsumerGroup, RebalanceCount, Severity = "High"
| order by RebalanceCount desc

// Detect partition fetch size exploitation
| KafkaMetrics
| where MetricName == "kafka.server.BrokerTopicMetrics.BytesInPerSec"
| where MetricValue > 100000000  // >100MB/sec
| summarize AvgFetchSize = avg(MetricValue), MaxFetchSize = max(MetricValue) by TopicName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where MaxFetchSize > 52428800  // Exceeds default fetch.max.bytes
| project TimeGenerated, TopicName, AvgFetchSize, MaxFetchSize, ThreatIndicator = "fetch.max.bytes exploitation"

event.dataset: "kafka.log" AND event.severity: ("error" OR "warn") AND message: (*rebalance* OR *consumer*group*)
| stats count() by kafka.consumer.group, bucket(timestamp, 1m)
| where count > 10

event.dataset: "kafka.metrics" AND kafka.server.topic.bytes_in_per_sec > 100000000
| stats avg(kafka.server.topic.bytes_in_per_sec) as avg_bytes, max(kafka.server.topic.bytes_in_per_sec) as max_bytes by kafka.topic
| where max_bytes > 52428800

index=kafka sourcetype=kafka:server:log (log_level=WARN OR log_level=ERROR) ("rebalance" OR "consumer group")
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count as rebalance_count by consumer_group, _time
| where rebalance_count > 10
| eval severity="HIGH", threat="Rebalance Storm Attack"
| table _time, consumer_group, rebalance_count, severity, threat

index=kafka sourcetype=kafka:metrics:jmx metric_name="kafka.server.BrokerTopicMetrics.BytesInPerSec"
| where metric_value > 100000000
| bin _time span=1m
| stats avg(metric_value) as avg_bytes, max(metric_value) as max_bytes by topic_name, _time
| where max_bytes > 52428800
| eval threat="fetch.max.bytes Exploitation"
| table _time, topic_name, avg_bytes, max_bytes, threat

# Consumer lag explosion detection
fields @timestamp, consumerGroup, lag, lagGrowthRate
| filter @message like /consumer.*lag/
| stats avg(lag) as avgLag, max(lag) as maxLag, (max(lag) - min(lag)) / 300 as lagGrowthRate by consumerGroup, bin(5m)
| filter maxLag > 10000 and lagGrowthRate > 1000
| sort lagGrowthRate desc

# Request handler saturation (offset commit flooding)
fields @timestamp, requestHandlerIdlePercent
| filter metricName = "RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent"
| stats avg(requestHandlerIdlePercent) as avgIdle by bin(1m)
| filter avgIdle < 0.20
| sort @timestamp desc

tools.name: Kafka Lag Exporter
tools.usage: Real-time consumer lag export to Prometheus for alerting
